summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1968 Florida festival set highlighted by a scorching Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven sequence.
review: The Miller‑taped soundboard delivers surprisingly clear vocals and a crisp mix for a 1968 recording, with only occasional vintage saturation. Musically the show is a powerhouse – Dark Star rolls straight into St. Stephen, bursts through The Eleven and The Other One, all backed by relentless energy that many reviewers call “steamroller” and “monster.”

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 4‑5 star reviews (16 substantive votes) praising both performance and audio quality; consistent mentions of standout songs and excellent guitar work.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear vocals, crisp mix and sharp guitar tones; occasional period‑era saturation but overall very listenable for a 1968 tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A seamless hour‑long set that flows from a tight Dark Star into St. Stephen, then rockets through The Eleven, The Other One and Cryptical, delivering constant momentum and crescendo.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solo work is repeatedly called blazing, crisp and “on fire,” especially on Dark Star, The Eleven and The Other One.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob is noted only for a humorous stage comment about the single; no performance critique.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten (TC) is described as “innocuous” and appears only sporadically.
